title: Piper portulacifolium
taxonomy: Piper portulacifolium is classified under the family Piperaceae. This family is a part of the order Piperales. The order Piperales is a part of the clade magnoliids, which is a group of flowering plants. Magnoliids are a part of the clade angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. The clade angiosperms is a part of the larger clade spermatophytes, which includes all seed-producing plants.
uses: Piper portulacifolium is known for its medicinal properties, which have been utilized in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Its extracts have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which can help combat infections and oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, Piper portulacifolium is also used to treat gastrointestinal issues and as a remedy for respiratory problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal applications.

constituents: Piper portulacifolium contains active constituents such as alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant's alkaloid profile is characterized by the presence of piperine, piperic acid, and other piperine derivatives. intro: Piper portulacifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Piperaceae, native to the tropical regions of the Western Ghats in India. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, which may help in reducing pain and inflammation associated with various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Piper portulacifolium include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
